title: The Confusion of Kaworu Nagisa
wikitext:
[]: The Confusion of Kaworu Nagisa             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Jan 10, 2013 8:34 pm I've come across this problem in every single thread I see about Kaworu on this site. Kaworu and Shinji, blah blah blah, he breaks into Terminal Dogma, blah blah blah, he opens Heaven's Gate. This is where the problems start flooding in. Well, that's a bit dramatic. There's only one problem. Why the hell did Kaworu ever confuse Lilith for Adam? Kaworu is an angel. Angels are attracted to Lilith. Kaworu knows this, becaue he feels the attraction. Why, then, is he so surprised upon breaking into Terminal Dogma? How could he have ever been confused as to the identity of the angel that he was seeking?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Jan 10, 2013 8:48 pm Maybe what ever instinct/"radar" the Angels have can't really differentiate between whatever "energy signatures" the SoLs have? The angels were *all* aiming for terminal Dogma, probably intending to find Adam there, (Those who weren't conducting research on human specimens first, that is) except for Gaghiel who probably just got lucky - and Misato noted that Gaghiel DID look like he was searching for something. Maybe Adam is harder to notice in his current mini form? In any case, Kaworu seems to have been the only one who got that they were all chasing the wrong target... And he may not have noticed at all if he had merged with it right away - He only found out in the first place because he was hesitant to wipe out the humans.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Thu Jan 10, 2013 9:12 pm ^ I have to agree with the Angel's not being able to tell either Adam or Lilith apart. Id imagine that both being SoL's that they put off the same "signature" as each other and that there is no real way to tell the difference besides actually laying eyes on it for visual conformation.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 10, 2013 9:41 pm Him mistaking Lilith for Adam was never really the problem. Him mistaking Lilith for Adam when he was Adam and was told exactly where his true body was is where the problem occurred. If I must fanwank something I shall go with he assumed Seele was trying to play him hence the betrayal and thought Gendo had some other plan only to realize it was the truth.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:47 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Him mistaking Lilith for Adam was never really the problem. Him mistaking Lilith for Adam when he was Adam and was told exactly where his true body was is where the problem occurred. If I must fanwank something I shall go with he assumed Seele was trying to play him hence the betrayal and thought Gendo had some other plan only to realize it was the truth.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:56 am Some of the previous go-arounds http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2095/The-Great-Episode-24-Plot-Hole-Fanwank-Thread/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11183/Filling-the-24-Plot-Hole-Once-and-for-All/

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:59 am Monk Ed wrote: I suspect that what he critically lacks is an S2, which he probably expected to find with the original body he was seeking. The fact that he was flying around and using ATF hax doesn't prove that he had one, because Rei in the same episode used her own ATF hax and showed up in a place she could have only gotten via flight.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Jan 11, 2013 2:01 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Kaworu talks about living forever with Unit 02, which also lacked an S2 engine, discourses about eternity always make me think about the S2 engine.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Jan 11, 2013 4:05 pm The same question would apply to Adam, since he was buried in Gendo's hand for the majority of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 4:15 pm The Adam embryo did have an S2 organ, just look at what Lilith did after Adam was absorbed. What it lacked was a soul and sufficient body mass.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 1:45 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: The Adam embryo did have an S2 organ, just look at what Lilith did after Adam was absorbed. What it lacked was a soul and sufficient body mass.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:56 am Reichu wrote: We see that the Angels, which are born from Adam with souls, have S2 Engines, and the Evas, which are (mostly) actual Adam clones and born without, do not.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 11:45 am Kendrix wrote: But the MP EVAS did have them
